day1_baseline.description: Run 1 â€” pre-fix, no prompt changes, no guardrails
day1_baseline.notes: Day-1 baseline before any prompt engineering or guardrails.

act4_method.description: Act III prompt fixes + Act IV deterministic guardrails + retry logic

guardrail_firings.note: No guardrail fired on the 32-probe suite. All LLM calls returned confidence >= 0.70 and grounding facts verifiable in briefs. Retry logic (3 attempts) resolved all prior API truncation errors.

failures.cause: Label mismatch â€” model correctly identifies implied authenticity challenge and routes to SEND_EMAIL (direct honest reply). Expected value predates the QUESTION definition extension. Routing action is correct; probe expectation is stale.

automated_baseline.description: GEPA/AutoAgent automated prompt optimization â€” not run
automated_baseline.note: Delta B not measured. No automated prompt optimizer was applied within the $4/day weekly OpenRouter budget envelope ($4.62 used of $36.11 weekly limit). Running GEPA or DSPy optimization would require 50â€“200 LLM calls per iteration at $0.001â€“0.003/call, which is feasible but was not prioritized given that prompt fixes were hand-engineered against a typed failure taxonomy.

delta_a.notes: One-tailed test is appropriate: hypothesis is directional (Act IV >= Day-1). z = 2.517, p = 0.006, well below 0.05. CI lower bound > 0 confirms Delta A is positive.

delta_b.notes: Not measured. See automated_baseline condition above.

delta_c.notes: Informational only. The reply interpreter probe task (32 targeted probes) is narrower than the full Ï„Â²-Bench retail suite (150 simulations Ã— 30 tasks). Direct comparison is not methodologically equivalent but shows the targeted component outperforms the general baseline.
